verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Now a certain man was sick, named Lazarus, of Bethany, of the town of Mary and her sister Martha.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was that Mary which anointed the Lord with ointment.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Many men, when they see any of those who are pleasing to God suffering anything terrible, as, for instance, having fallen into sickness, or poverty, and any other the like, are offended, not knowing that to those especially dear to God it belongeth to endure these things; since Lazarus also was one of the friends of Christ, and was sick.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This at least they who sent said, “Behold, he whom Thou lovest is sick.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But let us consider the passage from the beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “A certain man,” It saith, “was sick, Lazarus of Bethany.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not without a cause nor by chance hath the writer mentioned whence Lazarus was, but for a reason which he will afterwards tell us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At present let us keep to the passage before us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He also for our advantage informeth us who were Lazarus’ sisters; and, moreover, what Mary had more (than the other), going on to say, “It was that Mary which anointed the Lord with ointment.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here some doubting say, “How did the Lord endure that a woman should do this?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In the first place then it is necessary to understand, that this is not the harlot mentioned in Matthew ( Matt. xxvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 7 ), or the one in Luke ( Luke vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 37 ), but a different person; they were harlots full of many vices, but she was both grave and earnest; for she showed her earnestness about the entertainment of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Evangelist also means to show, that the sisters too loved Him, yet He allowed Lazarus to die.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why did they not, like the centurion and the nobleman, leave their sick brother, and come to Christ, instead of sending?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They were very confident in Christ, and had towards Him a strong familiar feeling.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, they were weak women, and oppressed with grief; for that they acted not in this way as thinking slightly of Him, they afterwards showed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is then clear, that this Mary was not the harlot.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But wherefore,” saith some one, “did Christ admit that harlot?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That He might put away her iniquity; that He might show His lovingkindness; that thou mightest learn that there is no malady which prevaileth over His goodness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Look not therefore at this only, that He received her, but consider the other point also, how He changed her.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But, (to return,) why doth the Evangelist relate this history to us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or rather, what doth he desire to show us by saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus loved Martha, and her sister, and Lazarus.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That we should never be discontented or vexed if any sickness happen to good men, and such as are dear to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Behold, he whom thou lovest is sick.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They desired to draw on Christ to pity, for they still gave heed to Him as to a man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is plain from what they say, “If thou hadst been here, he had not died,” and from their saying, not, “Behold, Lazarus is sick,” but “Behold, he whom thou lovest is sick.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then said Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory of God, that the Son of God might be glorified thereby.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe how He again asserteth that His glory and the Father’s is One; for after saying “of God,” He hath added, “that the Son of God might be glorified.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “This sickness is not unto death.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since He intended to tarry two days where He was, He for the present sendeth away the messengers with this answer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore we must admire Lazarus’ sisters, that after hearing that the sickness was “not unto death,” and yet seeing him dead, they were not offended, although the event had been directly contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But even so they came to Him, and did not think that He had spoken falsely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The expression “that” in this passage denotes not cause, but consequence; the sickness happened from other causes, but He used it for the glory of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And having said this, He tarried two days.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore tarried He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That Lazarus might breathe his last, and be buried; that none might be able to assert that He restored him when not yet dead, saying that it was a lethargy, a fainting, a fit, but not death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account He tarried so long, that corruption began, and they said, “He now stinketh.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then saith He to his disciples, Let us go into Judea.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why, when He never in other places told them beforehand where He was going, doth He tell them here?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They had been greatly terrified, and since they were in this way disposed, He forewarneth them, that the suddenness might not trouble them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then say the disciples?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The Jews of late sought to stone Thee, and goest Thou thither again?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They therefore had feared for Him also, but for the more part rather for themselves; for they were not yet perfect.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So Thomas, shaking with fear, said, “Let us go, that we also may die with Him” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 16 ), because Thomas was weaker and more unbelieving than the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But see how Jesus encourageth them by what He saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Are there not twelve hours of the day?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He either saith this, that “he who is conscious to himself of no evil, shall suffer nothing dreadful; only he that doeth evil shall suffer, so that we need not fear, because we have done nothing worthy of death”; or else that, “he who ‘seeth the light of this world’ is in safety; and if he that seeth the light of this world is in safety, much more he that is with Me, if he separate not himself from Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Having encouraged them by these words, He addeth, that the cause of their going thither was pressing, and showeth them that they were about to go not unto Jerusalem, but unto Bethany.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Our friend Lazarus,” He saith, “sleepeth, but I go that I may awake him out of sleep.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “I go not for the same purpose as before, again to reason and contend with the Jews, but to awaken our friend.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then said His disciples, Lord, if he sleep he shall do well.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This they said not without a cause, but desiring to hinder the going thither.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Sayest Thou,” asks one of them, “that he sleepeth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then there is no urgent reason for going.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet on this account He had said, “Our friend,” to show that the going there was necessary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore their disposition was somewhat reluctant, He said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He is dead.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The former word He spake, desiring to prove that He loved not boasting; but since they understood not, He added, “He is dead.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And I am glad for your sakes.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why “for your sakes”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Because I have forewarned you of his death, not being there, and because when I shall raise him again, there will be no suspicion of deceit.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how the disciples were yet imperfect in their disposition, and knew not His power as they ought? and this was caused by interposing terrors, which troubled and disturbed their souls.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When He said, “He sleepeth,” He added, “I go to awake him”; but when He said, “He is dead,” He added not, “I go to raise him”; for He would not foretell in words what He was about to establish certainly by works, everywhere teaching us not to be vainglorious, and that we must not make promises without a cause.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if He did thus in the case of the centurion when summoned, (for He said, “I will come and heal him Matt. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 7 ,) it was to show the faith of the centurion that He said this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If any one ask, “How did the disciples imagine sleep?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How did they not understand that death was meant from His saying, ‘I go to awake him?’ for it was folly if they expected that He would go fifteen stadia to awake him”; we would reply, that they deemed this to be a dark saying, such as He often spake to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now they all feared the attacks of the Jews, but Thomas above the rest; wherefore also he said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Let us go, that we also may die with Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Some say that he desired himself to die; but it is not so; the expression is rather one of cowardice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet he was not rebuked, for Christ as yet supported his weakness, but afterwards he became stronger than all, and invincible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the wonderful thing is this; that we see one who was so weak before the Crucifixion, become after the Crucifixion, and after having believed in the Resurrection, more zealous than any.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So great was the power of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The very man who dared not go in company with Christ to Bethany, the same while not seeing Christ ran well nigh through the inhabited world, and dwelt in the midst of nations that were full of murder, and desirous to kill him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if Bethany was “fifteen furlongs off,” which is two miles, how was Lazarus “dead four days”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Jesus tarried two days, on the day before those two one had come with the message, (on which same day Lazarus died,) then in the course of the fourth day He arrived.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He waited to be summoned, and came not uninvited on this account, that no one might suspect what took place; nor did those women who were beloved by Him come themselves, but others were sent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Now Bethany was about fifteen furlongs off.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not without cause doth he mention this, but desires to inform us that it was near, and that it was probable on this account that many would be there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He therefore declaring this adds,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Many of the Jews came to comfort them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how should they comfort women beloved of Christ, when they had agreed, that if any should confess Christ, he should be put out of the synagogue?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was either because of the grievous nature of the calamity, or that they respected them as of superior birth, or else these who came were not the wicked sort, many at least even of them believed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Evangelist mentions these circumstances, to prove that Lazarus was really dead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why did not [Martha,] when she went to meet Christ, take her sister with her?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: She desired to meet with Him apart, and to tell Him what had taken place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when He had brought her to good hopes, she went and called Mary, who met Him while her grief was yet at its height.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how fervent her love was?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is the Mary of whom He said, “Mary hath chosen that good part.” ( Luke x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How then,” saith one, “doth Martha appear more zealous?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: She was not more zealous, but it was because the other had not yet been informed, since Martha was the weaker.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For even when she had heard such things from Christ, she yet speaks in a groveling manner, “By this time he stinketh, for he hath been dead four days.” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But Mary, though she had heard nothing, uttered nothing of the kind, but at once believing, saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Lord, if Thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: See how great is the heavenly wisdom of the women, although their understanding be weak.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when they saw Christ, they did not break out into mourning and wailing and loud crying, as we do when we see any of those we know coming in upon our grief; but straightway they reverence their Teacher.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So then both these sisters believed in Christ, but not in a right way; for they did not yet certainly know either that He was God, or that He did these things by His own power and authority; on both which points He taught them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they showed their ignorance of the former, by saying, “If thou hadst been here, our brother had not died”; and of the latter, by saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Whatsoever thou wilt ask of God, He will give it thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As though they spoke of some virtuous and approved mortal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But see what Christ saith;

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thy brother shall rise again.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He thus far refuteth the former saying, “Whatsoever thou wilt ask”; for He said not, “I ask,” but what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thy brother shall rise again.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To have said, “Woman, thou still lookest below, I need not the help of another, but do all of Myself,” would have been grievous, and a stumblingblock in her way, but to say, “He shall rise again,” was the act of one who chose a middle mode of speech.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And by means of that which follows, He alluded to the points I have mentioned; for when Martha saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I know that he shall rise again in the last day,” to prove more clearly His authority, He replieth,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I am the Resurrection and the Life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Showing that He needed no other to help Him, if so be that He Himself is the Life; since if He needed another, how could He be “the Resurrection and the Life”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet He did not plainly state this, but merely hinted it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when she saith again, “Whatsoever thou wilt ask,” He replieth,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that believeth in Me, though he were dead, yet shall he live.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Showing that He is the Giver of good things, and that we must ask of Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And whosoever liveth and believeth in Me, shall never die.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe how He leadeth her mind upward; for to raise Lazarus was not the only thing sought; it was necessary that both she and they who were with her should learn the Resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore before the raising of the dead He teacheth heavenly wisdom by words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if He is “the Resurrection,” and “the Life,” He is not confined by place, but, present everywhere, knoweth how to heal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If therefore they had said, as did the centurion, “Speak the word, and my servant shall be healed” ( Matt. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 8 ), He would have done so; but since they summoned Him to them, and begged Him to come, He condescendeth in order to raise them from the humble opinion they had formed of Him, and cometh to the place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Still while condescending, He showed that even when absent He had power to heal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account also He delayed, for the mercy would not have been appar ent as soon as it was given, had there not been first an ill savor (from the corpse).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how did the woman know that there was to be a Resurrection?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They had heard Christ say many things about the Resurrection, yet still she now desired to see Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe how she still lingers below; for after hearing, “I am the Resurrection and the Life,” not even so did she say, “Raise him,” but,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I believe that Thou art the Christ, the Son of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is Christ’s reply?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that believeth on Me, though he were dead, yet shall he live,” (here speaking of this death which is common to all.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And whosoever liveth and believeth on Me, shall never die” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 26 ), signifying that other death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Since then I am the Resurrection and the Life, be not thou troubled, though thy brother be already dead, but believe, for this is not death.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For a while He comforted her on what had happened; and gave her glimpses of hope, by saying, “He shall rise again,” and, “I am the Resurrection”; and that having risen again, though he should again die, he shall suffer no harm, so that it needs not to fear this death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He saith is of this kind: “Neither is this man dead, nor shall ye die.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Believest thou this?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: She saith, “I believe that Thou art the Christ, the Son of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Which should come into the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The woman seems to me not to understand the saying; she was conscious that it was some great thing, but did not perceive the whole meaning, so that when asked one thing, she answered another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet for a while at least she had this gain, that she moderated her grief; such was the power of the words of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account Martha went forth first, and Mary followed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For their affection to their Teacher did not allow them strongly to feel their present sorrow; so that the minds of these women were truly wise as well as loving.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But in our days, among our other evils there is one malady very prevalent among our women; they make a great show in their dirges and wailings, baring their arms, tearing their hair, making furrows down their cheeks.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this they do, some from grief, others from ostentation and rivalry, others from wantonness; and they bare their arms, and this too in the sight of men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why doest thou, woman?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou strip thyself in unseemly sort, tell me, thou who art a member of Christ, in the midst of the market-place, when men are present there?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou pluck thy hair, and rend thy garments, and wail loudly, and join the dance, and keep throughout a resemblance to Bacchanalian women, and dost thou not think that thou art offending God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What madness is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Will not the heathen laugh?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Will they not deem our doctrines fables?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They will say, “There is no resurrection—the doctrines of the Christians are mockeries, trickery, and contrivance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For their women lament as though there were nothing after this world; they give no heed to the words engraven in their books; all those words are fictions, and these women show that they are so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since had they believed that he who hath died is not dead, but hath removed to a better life, they would not have mourned him as no longer being, they would not have thus beaten themselves, they would not have uttered such words as these, full of unbelief, ‘I shall never see thee more, I shall never more regain thee,’ all their religion is a fable, and if the very chief of good things is thus wholly disbelieved by them, much more the other things which are reverenced among them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The heathen are not so womanish, among them many have practiced heavenly wisdom; and a woman hearing that her child had fallen in battle, straightway asked, “And in what state are the affairs of the city?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Another truly wise, when being garlanded he heard that his son had fallen for his country, took off the garland, and asked which of the two; then when he had learnt which it was, immediately put the garland on again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Many also gave their sons and their daughters for slaughter in honor of their evil deities; and Lacedæmonian women exhort their sons either to bring back their shield safe from war, or to be brought back dead upon it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore I am ashamed that the heathen show true wisdom in these matters, and we act unseemly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Those who know nothing about the Resurrection act the part of those who know; and those who know, the part of those who know not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And ofttimes many do through shame of men what they do not for the sake of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For women of the higher class neither tear their hair nor bare their arms; which very thing is a most heavy charge against them, not because they do not strip themselves, but because they act as they do not through piety, but that they may not be thought to disgrace themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is their shame stronger than grief, and the fear of God not stronger?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And must not this deserve severest censure?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What the rich women do because of their riches, the poor ought to do through fear of God; but at present it is quite the contrary; the rich act wisely through vainglory, the poor through littleness of soul act unseemly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is worse than this anomaly?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We do all for men, all for the things of earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And these people utter words full of madness and much ridicule.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Lord saith indeed, “Blessed are they that mourn” ( Matt. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 4 ), speaking of those who mourn for their sins; and no one mourneth that kind of mourning, nor careth for a lost soul; but this other we were not bidden to practice, and we practice it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What then?” saith some one, “Is it possible being man not to weep?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: No, neither do I forbid weeping, but I forbid the beating yourselves, the weeping immoderately.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I am neither brutal nor cruel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I know that our nature asks and seeks for its friends and daily companions; it cannot but be grieved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As also Christ showed, for He wept over Lazarus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So do thou; weep, but gently, but with decency, but with the fear of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If so thou weepest, thou dost so not as disbelieving the Resurrection, but as not enduring the separation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since even over those who are leaving us, and departing to foreign lands, we weep, yet we do this not as despairing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so do thou weep, as if thou wert sending one on his way to another land.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These things I say, not as giving a rule of action, but as condescending (to human infirmity).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if the dead man have been a sinner, and one who hath in many things offended God, it behooveth to weep (or rather not to weep only, since that is of no avail to him, but to do what one can to procure some comfort for him by almsgivings and offerings;) but it behooveth also to rejoice at this, that his wickedness hath been cut short.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If he have been righteous, it again behooveth to be glad, that what is his is now placed in security, free from the uncertainty of the future; if young, that he hath been quickly delivered from the common evils of life; if old, that he hath departed after taking to satiety that which is held desirable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But thou, neglecting to consider these things, incitest thy hand-maidens to act as mourners, as if forsooth thou wert honoring the dead, when it is an act of extreme dishonor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For honor to the dead is not wailings and lamentings, but hymns and psalmodies and an excellent life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The good man when he departeth, shall depart with angels, though no man be near his remains; but the corrupt, though he have a city to attend his funeral, shall be nothing profited.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wilt thou honor him who is gone?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Honor him in another way, by alms-deeds, by acts of beneficence and public service.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What avail the many lamentations?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And I have heard also another grievous thing, that many women attract lovers by their sad cries, acquiring by the fervor of their wailings a reputation for affection to their husbands.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: O devilish purpose!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: O Satanic invention!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How long are we but dust and ashes, how long but blood and flesh?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Look we up to heaven, take we thought of spiritual things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How shall we be able to rebuke the heathen, how to exhort them, when we do such things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How shall we dispute with them concerning the Resurrection?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How about the rest of heavenly wisdom?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How shall we ourselves live without fear?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Knowest not thou that of grief cometh death? for grief darkening the seeing part of the soul not only hindereth it from perceiving anything that it ought, but also worketh it great mischief.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In one way then we offend God, and advantage neither ourselves nor him who is gone; in the other we please God, and gain honor among men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If we sink not down ourselves, He will soon remove the remains of our despondency; if we are discontented, He permitteth us to be given up to grief.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If we are thankful, we shall not despond.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But how,” saith some one, “is it possible not to be grieved, when one has lost a son or daughter or wife?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I say not, “not to grieve,” but “not to do so immoderately.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if we consider that God hath taken away, and that the husband or son which we had was mortal, we shall soon receive comfort.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To be discontented is the act of those who seek for something higher than their nature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou wast born man, and mortal; why then grievest thou that what is natural hath come to pass?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Grievest thou that thou art nourished by eating?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seekest thou to live without this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Act thus also in the case of death, and being mortal seek not as yet for immortality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Once for all this thing hath been appointed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Grieve not therefore, nor play the mourner, but submit to laws laid on all alike.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Grieve for thy sins; this is good mourning, this is highest wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then mourn for this cause continually, that we may obtain the joy which is there, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory for ever and ever.
